MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. AMY BETH CLARK, Esq. of Beverly Law Firm (tel: 310-552-6959) for Plaintiff.
Atty. RODRIGO BOZOGHLIAN, Esq. of Lagasse Branch Bell + Kinkead LLP (tel: 213-817-9152) for Defendant Anthony Drayton.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ANTHONY DRAYTON's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter discussed. Counsel Clark requests to serve defendant via publication. The Court instructs Consel Clark to bring a motion before the Court. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Specially appearing defendant Anthony Drayton's amended motion to quash plaintiff's substituted service of the summons and complaint" is granted.

Plaintiff purports to have served Drayton by leaving copies at his dwelling/abode per CCP 415.20(b). (Opp. 6:24-25.) However, Drayton declares that he has not "been present at this address for at least" six months and only "occasionally stayed" there "for short periods of time in the past." (Drayton Dec. 2:7-11.) Plaintiff's opposition adduces no evidence to the contrary. (Id. at 6:13-4:14.)

Order signed.
